rationale: Structural mutation of a persistent, shared on-disk data structure (B+Tree). Delete's rebalancing (merge/redistribute/splice/root-collapse) touches multiple nodes across levels in a single logical operation; any bug risks silent data loss (orphaned/dangling child pointers, broken NextLeaf chains, lost keys) that would not necessarily surface until a later Lookup/scan/persist subtask. No prior subtask in this epic performed multi-node structural rewrites in one operation (Insert's split only ever adds nodes and appends one key per ancestor level; Delete can remove nodes/keys and re-point pointers).

files_touched.reason: New Delete implementation: leaf key removal, leaf-level borrow/merge repair, internal-level grandparent-splice repair, root collapse.
Test coverage per issue's TestDelete acceptance spec, plus a no-orphaned-pointers structural checker built on real Insert-built trees.

downstream_dependents: engine/btree/scan.go (subtask 1.2.5, prefix scan) will rely on NextLeaf chain integrity that Delete's merge/splice logic must preserve

engine/btree/btree_test.go (subtask 1.2.6, persist/reload) will need to account for Delete's documented known gap: abandoned node IDs are never reclaimed by NodeAllocator's free-list-less monotonic allocator
backward_compatibility: Purely additive: no existing exported symbol's signature or behavior changed. Insert, Lookup, NodeStore, NodeAllocator, node encode/decode all untouched.
known_gaps_documented_in_code: Abandoned/merged-away node IDs are never reused or reclaimed (no free-list); expected to be revisited alongside 1.2.5/1.2.6 persist/reload work.

Leaf-level key-borrow can, in principle, replace a parent's separator key with a longer string than the original, which could theoretically overflow the parent's encoded size; not handled (accepted as out of scope for this subtask's uniform-length-key test spec).

Underflow threshold is 'completely empty of keys' (tombstone-until-empty policy) rather than a numeric half-full threshold, since docs/LLD/btree.md does not specify one and the issue explicitly permits a 'documented simplified' strategy.
